name: overnight_with_adaptive_setback_and_wakeup
description: Warm summer day: afternoon cooling (14:00–22:00), bedtime at 22:30 applies P3 sleep band (ceiling=DEFAULT_SLEEP_COOL=72F), morning wakeup at 07:00 restores comfort. Full overnight cycle.
notes: P3 migration (Issue #249): bedtime no longer uses comfort_cool + DEFAULT_SETBACK_DEPTH_COOL_F.
P3 uses select_comfort_band(in_sleep_window=True): ceiling = sleep_cool or DEFAULT_SLEEP_COOL = 72F.

DEFAULT_SLEEP_COOL = 72.0 (const.py) — reformatted to a real tuned installation's own flat overnight value (architecture-reset session). With comfort_cool=74, this is 2F BELOW comfort ceiling: this household sleeps cooler, not warmer.
No thermal model configured — uses default sleep band path.

NOTE: expect_temp updated from 77F (old formula) to 78F (DEFAULT_SLEEP_COOL in P3), then to 72F when DEFAULT_SLEEP_COOL was reformatted to match a real household's own configured overnight preference (Issue #435/#436).

events.note: Afternoon classification — cool mode starts at comfort_cool=74F
Evening: indoor cooling down, outdoor still warm — AC running normally
Bedtime: cool setback fires. DEFAULT_SLEEP_COOL=72F (no explicit sleep_cool configured) — cooler than daytime comfort_cool=74F.
Overnight: indoor 76.5F with setback target 72F — AC runs to reach the cooler sleep target

assertions.reason: Afternoon classification: warm day, comfort band armed.
P3: comfort band ceiling=comfort_cool=74F confirms cool defense target at classification time.

P3 bedtime: sleep band ceiling = DEFAULT_SLEEP_COOL = 72F (active=ceiling, warm night). This household sleeps cooler than daytime comfort (74F), not warmer — a real, tuned installation's own preference (Issue #435/#436).

verdict.summary: P3 overnight cycle: cool classification → sleep band (72F ceiling) → morning comfort restore (74F)
verdict.observed_behavior: classification_applied (74F) at 14:00; setback_applied (band ceiling=72F) at 22:30; comfort_restored (74F) at 07:00
verdict.expected_behavior: P3: sleep band [64/72] active=ceiling. Occupant sleeps in home set to a 72F ceiling — cooler than daytime comfort 74F, matching this household's real preference to sleep cooler. Band floor 64F guards against overcooling during the night.
